volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: The Little Carthaginian (Section 3.3.638 - Section 3.4.716)

sections.label: Section 3.3.638
sections.original: Quia nos honoris tui causa ad te venimus ,
sections.translation: Because for the sake of your own well-doing we came hither , although in a very moderate degree do we entertain good wishes for procurers .

sections.label: Section 3.3.640
sections.original: Si quid boni adportatis , habeo gratiam .
sections.translation: If you bring anything that ’ s good , I give you thanks .

sections.label: Section 3.3.641
sections.original: Boni de nostro tibi nec ferimus nec damus
sections.translation: Of our own , we neither bring nor give you anything that ’ s good , nor do we promise you , nor , in fact , do we wish to give it .

sections.label: Section 3.3.643
sections.original: Credo hercle vobis : ita vestra est benignitas .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I do believe yon ; such is your kindly feeling . But what now do you wish ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.644b
sections.translation: This person in the scarf , whom you see ,

sections.label: Section 3.3.645
sections.translation: with him Mars is angered .

sections.label: Section 3.3.645b
sections.translation: May he be so indeed with your own heads !

sections.label: Section 3.3.646
sections.original: Nunc hunc , Lyce , ad te diripiundum adducimus .
sections.translation: We are now bringing him here , Lycus , to you , for tearing asunder [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.3.647
sections.original: Cum praeda hic hodie incedet venator domum :
sections.translation: This huntsman , myself , will be going home to-day with some spoil ; the dogs are cleverly driving Lycus into the toils .

sections.label: Section 3.3.649
sections.translation: Who is this person ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.649b
sections.original: Nescimus nos quidem istum qui siet ;
sections.translation: We really don ’ t know who he is , except that some time since , after daybreak , when we went down to the harbour ,

sections.label: Section 3.3.650
sections.original: nisi dudum mane ut ad portum processimus ,
sections.translation: at the same moment we saw him landing from a merchant-ship . Disembarking , he came up to us at once — he saluted us ; we answered him .

sections.label: Section 3.3.653b
sections.translation: The artful fellows ! how cleverly they do enter upon the plot !

sections.label: Section 3.3.655
sections.translation: What after that ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.655b
sections.translation: Then he joined in discourse with us : he said that he was a foreigner , unacquainted with this city : that he wanted a convenient place to be found here , for him to indulge his appetite . We brought the man to you ; if the Gods are favourable to you , it ’ s an opportunity for you to ply your trade .

sections.label: Section 3.3.660
sections.translation: Is he eager to that degree ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.660b
sections.translation: He has got gold .

sections.label: Section 3.3.660c
sections.translation: That booty is mine .

sections.label: Section 3.3.661
sections.translation: He wishes to drink and wench .

sections.label: Section 3.3.661b
sections.translation: I ’ ll find him a nice place .

sections.label: Section 3.3.662
sections.original: At enim hic clam furtim ésse volt , ne quis sciat
sections.translation: But still he wants to be quite private , in a quiet way , that no persons may know it , and that there may be no overlookers ; for he has been a soldier in Sparta , as , indeed , he himself has told us , with King Attalus [*] ;

sections.label: Section 3.3.665
sections.original: inde huc aufugit , quoniam capitur oppidum .
sections.translation: from there he fled hither , when the town was surrendered .

sections.label: Section 3.3.666
sections.original: Nimis lépide de latrone , de Sparta optume .
sections.translation: Very clever that , about the soldier ! about Sparta , most capital !

sections.label: Section 3.3.667
sections.original: Di deaeque vobis multa bona dent , quom mihi
sections.translation: May the Gods and Goddesses bestow many blessings on you , for having given me kindly information , and finding me a choice prey .

sections.label: Section 3.3.669
sections.original: Immo ut ipse nobis dixit , quo accures magis ,
sections.translation: Aye , and , as he himself has told us , that you may receive him the better ,

sections.label: Section 3.3.670
sections.translation: he has brought three hundred Philippean pieces as a provision .

sections.label: Section 3.3.671
sections.original: Rex sum , si ego illum hodie ad me hominem adlexero .
sections.translation: I ’ m a king if I can to-day entice this man to my house .

sections.label: Section 3.3.672
sections.translation: Nay but , he really is your own .

sections.label: Section 3.3.672b
sections.translation: By my troth , prithee , do persuade him to take up his abode at my house , as the best lodging .

sections.label: Section 3.3.674
sections.original: Neque nos hortari neque dehortari decet
sections.translation: It befit us neither to persuade nor to dissuade

sections.label: Section 3.3.675
sections.original: hominem peregrinum : tuam rem tú ages , si sapis .
sections.translation: a person who is a foreigner ; you ’ ll transact your own business , if you are prudent . We have brought the ringdove for you , even to the trapping-ground ; now it ’ s better for yourself to catch him , if you wish him to be caught .

sections.label: Section 3.3.678
sections.original: Iamne itis ? quid quod vobis mandavi , hospites ?
sections.translation: Are you going now ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.679
sections.original: Cum illoc te meliust tuam rem , ádulescens , loqui :
sections.translation: It ’ s better for you , young sir , to speak to him about your own concerns ;

sections.label: Section 3.3.680
sections.original: illic ést ad istas res probus , quas quaeritas .
sections.translation: he ’ s clever in those matters which you are enquiring about .

sections.label: Section 3.3.681
sections.original: Videre equidem vos vellem , quom huic aurum darem .
sections.translation: But , for my part , I could like you to see when I deliver him the gold .

sections.label: Section 3.3.682
sections.translation: At a distance there we shall be witnesses of that .

sections.label: Section 3.3.683
sections.translation: You ’ ve given me kind assistance .

sections.label: Section 3.3.683b
sections.translation: The profit comes to me .

sections.label: Section 3.3.684
sections.original: Illud quidem quorsum asinus caedit calcibus .
sections.translation: Age , just so , indeed , the way that the ass kicks [*] with his heels .

sections.label: Section 3.3.685
sections.translation: I ’ ll speak the fellow fairly . ( To COLLYBISCUS .) A stranger salutes a stranger ; I ’ m glad that you have arrived in safety .

sections.label: Section 3.3.687
sections.original: Multa tibi di dent bona , quom me salvom esse vis .
sections.translation: May the Deities grant you many blessings , since you wish me well .

sections.label: Section 3.3.688
sections.translation: They say that you are in search of a lodging .

sections.label: Section 3.3.688b
sections.translation: I am in search .

sections.label: Section 3.3.689
sections.original: Ita illí dixerunt , qui hinc a me abierunt modo ,
sections.translation: So those persons told me , who left me just now ,

sections.label: Section 3.3.690
sections.translation: that you are in search of one that is free from flies .

sections.label: Section 3.3.690b
sections.translation: By no means in the world .

sections.label: Section 3.3.691
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.691b
sections.original: Quia a muscis si mi hospitium quaererem ,
sections.translation: Because if I had been looking for a retreat from the flies [*] , on arriving here I should have straightway gone to gaol . I ’ m in search of this kind of lodging , where I may be treated more delicately than the eyes of King Antiochus [*] are in the habit of being treated .

sections.label: Section 3.3.695
sections.original: Edepol ne tibi illud possum festivom dare ,
sections.translation: Upon my faith , for sure , I can provide you a charming one , if , indeed , you can put up with yourself being in a charming room , on a couch charmingly laid , a charming damsel cuddling you .

sections.label: Section 3.3.698b
sections.translation: You are in the right road , Procurer .

sections.label: Section 3.3.699
sections.original: Vbi tu Leucadio , Lesbio , Thasio , Chio ,
sections.translation: Where , with Leucadian , Lesbian , Thasian , and Coan

sections.label: Section 3.3.700
sections.translation: wine [*] , toothless with old age [*] , you may soak yourself . There I ’ ll quite drench you with the effusion of unguents . Why many words ? I ’ ll cause , when you ’ ve bathed , the bathkeeper to set up unguent-shop there . But ( speaking confidentially ) all these things that I have mentioned let out their services for pay .

sections.label: Section 3.3.705
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.705b
sections.translation: Because they demand ready money .

sections.label: Section 3.3.706
sections.original: Quin hercle accipere tu non mavis quam ego dare .
sections.translation: Why , upon my faith , you are not more ready to receive than I to give .

sections.label: Section 3.3.707
sections.original: Quid si evocemus huc foras Agorastoclem ,
sections.translation: What if we call Agorastocles hither out of doors , that he himself may be his own witness , past all exception ? ( They go to the door of AGORASTOCLES and call out , in a loud whisper . Hallo ! you that are to catch the thief , come out quickly , that you yourself may witness him giving the gold to the Procurer .

sections.label: Section 3.4.711
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ? What is it you want , witnesses ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.711b
sections.translation: Look to the right hand ; your servant is paying gold to the Procurer himself .

sections.label: Section 3.4.713
sections.original: Age , accipe hoc sis : hic sunt numerati aurei
sections.translation: Come , take this , will you : here are three hundred gold coins , counted out , which are called Philippeans . ( Gives him the bay .) With these do you provide for me . I wish these to be spent with all speed .

sections.label: Section 3.4.716
sections.translation: By my troth , you have found a lavish steward for yourself . Come , let ’ s away in-doors .
